extraction.one_line_summary: BETTIOL Alberto attacked from 23-rider breakaway on penultimate climb, caught CAVAGNA Rémi solo on final ascent, won by 17 seconds over chasing group.
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA Rémi's solo attack gained 30 seconds initially but cracked completely when caught, suggesting diesel riders can't sustain punchy climb surges.
BETTIOL Alberto waited until penultimate climb to bridge despite 30-second gap, demonstrating classics riders should trust power reserves over early panic.
23-rider breakaway took 50km to establish on longest Giro stage, revealing peloton fatigue creates delayed selection on flat transition stages.

rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: positive: Won from large breakaway by timing bridge on penultimate climb, showing classics engine translates to hilly breakaway stages.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: negative: Built 30-second solo gap but completely emptied when caught, exposed weakness sustaining efforts after early acceleration.
rider_signals.CONSONNI Simone: positive: Finished second from breakaway chase group, demonstrating surprising climbing range for perceived sprinter type.
rider_signals.ROCHES Nicolas: positive: Bridged to leaders and held third despite being dropped once, showing veteran resilience on undulating finales.
rider_signals.BEVIN Patrick: neutral: Launched early attack from 23-rider break but faded, suggesting premature acceleration from large groups rarely succeeds.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: Retained maglia rosa by 23+ minutes as expected on breakaway stage with no GC implications.
